jsonLdWeb.name: Spray Foam Insulation Calculator
jsonLdWeb.description: Calculate board feet, thickness, kits, and cost for spray foam insulation projects.

mainEntity.name: What is a board foot of spray foam?

acceptedAnswer.text: A board foot is a unit of volume equal to 1 square foot of coverage at 1 inch thick (12" × 12" × 1"). To calculate board feet, multiply the area in square feet by the thickness in inches. For example, 1,000 sq ft at 3 inches thick = 3,000 board feet.

mainEntity.name: What R-value per inch for spray foam?

acceptedAnswer.text: Open-cell spray foam provides R-3.5 per inch. Closed-cell spray foam provides R-6.5 per inch. To reach R-38 (attic standard), open-cell needs about 11 inches, while closed-cell needs about 6 inches.

mainEntity.name: How much does spray foam insulation cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: Open-cell foam costs $0.35–$0.55 per board foot installed. Closed-cell foam costs $1.00–$1.50 per board foot installed. DIY kits cost less per board foot but require equipment rental. A 1,000 sq ft wall at R-13 costs roughly $1,300–$2,000 for open-cell or $2,000–$3,000 for closed-cell.

mainEntity.name: Open-cell vs closed-cell: which should I use?

acceptedAnswer.text: Closed-cell is denser, acts as a moisture barrier, and adds structural strength — ideal for crawl spaces, basements, and exterior walls. Open-cell is softer, cheaper, better for soundproofing, and ideal for interior walls and attics. Closed-cell is required below grade.

mainEntity.name: How many spray foam kits do I need?

acceptedAnswer.text: DIY spray foam kits come in various sizes: small kits cover 200–600 board feet, large kits cover 600–1,200 board feet. Divide your total board feet by the kit coverage. Professional installers use bulk equipment, not kits.

mainEntity.name: How thick should spray foam be?

acceptedAnswer.text: Thickness depends on the target R-value. Divide your desired R-value by R-per-inch: for R-13 walls with closed-cell, 13 ÷ 6.5 = 2 inches. For R-38 attic with open-cell, 38 ÷ 3.5 = 10.9 inches. Most building codes specify minimum R-values by climate zone.

howItWorks:
How to Calculate Spray Foam Insulation

First determine the thickness needed from R-value and foam type, then multiply by area to get board feet.

Board Feet = Area (sq ft) × Thickness (inches)
  • Thickness: Desired R-value ÷ R-value per inch
  • Open-cell: R-3.5 per inch — $0.35–$0.55/board ft installed
  • Closed-cell: R-6.5 per inch — $1.00–$1.50/board ft installed
  • Board foot: 1 sq ft at 1 inch thick (12" × 12" × 1")
  • DIY kits: ~600 board feet per large kit
  • Add 10–15% for waste, overspray, and irregular surfaces
faqHTML:
Frequently Asked Questions

What is a board foot of spray foam?

1 sq ft at 1 inch thick. Multiply area (sq ft) by thickness (inches) to get board feet. 1,000 sq ft at 3" thick = 3,000 board feet.

What R-value per inch?

Open-cell: R-3.5/inch. Closed-cell: R-6.5/inch. Closed-cell provides nearly double the insulation per inch.

How much does spray foam cost?

Open-cell: $0.35-$0.55/board ft installed. Closed-cell: $1.00-$1.50/board ft. A 1,000 sq ft wall at R-13 costs $1,300-$3,000 depending on type.

Open-cell vs closed-cell?

Closed-cell for moisture barriers, structural strength, below-grade. Open-cell for soundproofing, interior walls, attics, and lower cost.

How many kits do I need?

Large DIY kits cover ~600 board feet. Divide total board feet by 600. For large projects, professional bulk installation is more economical.

How thick should spray foam be?

Divide target R-value by R-per-inch. R-13 with closed-cell = 2". R-38 with open-cell = 10.9". Check local codes for minimum R-values.

sections.title: What Is the Spray Foam Insulation Calculator?
sections.body: The Spray Foam Insulation Calculator is a free online tool designed for contractors and DIY builders who need quick, accurate calculations in the construction and building space. By entering your area to insulate, foam type, desired r-value, you get instant results including thickness needed, board feet, diy kits. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting thickness needed right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In construction and building, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by contractors and DIY builders worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to plan your projects with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your area to insulate and need to find the right thickness needed. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Area to Insulate (sq ft)
howToSteps.description: Start by entering your area to insulate — this is the primary input for the calculation.

howToSteps.title: Fill In Additional Details
howToSteps.description: Complete the remaining fields: foam type, desired r-value, cost per board foot. Each value refines the calculation for greater accuracy.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your thickness needed, board feet, diy kits. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your area to insulate before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.

faq.question: Is the Spray Foam Insulation Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard construction and building formulas trusted by contractors and DIY builders. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

faq.question: What area to insulate should I enter?
faq.answer: Enter the most accurate area to insulate value you have available. If you're estimating, use a conservative figure. You can always run the calculator again with different values to see how changes affect the results.

faq.question: Can I use this calculator on my phone?
faq.answer: Yes, the Spray Foam Insulation Calculator is fully responsive and works on any device — phone, tablet, or desktop. No app download needed.

faq.question: What does thickness needed mean in my results?
faq.answer: The thickness needed value represents the primary output of the calculation based on your inputs. Use it as a baseline figure for your construction and building planning and decision-making.
